questions.question: Explain the difference between while and do-while loops with examples.
keyPoints: while loop checks condition first, may not execute body
do-while executes body at least once before checking condition
Example of while: int i=0; while(i<3) { i++; }
Example of do-while: do { i++; } while(i<3);

questions.question: What is the purpose of break and continue statements in loops? Give examples.
keyPoints: continue skips current iteration and moves to next

questions.question: What is an enhanced for loop? When should it be used?
keyPoints: Used to iterate over arrays and collections
Use when index is not needed

questions.question: Explain labeled break and labeled continue with an example.
keyPoints: Labeled break exits specific outer loop
Labeled continue jumps to specific outer loop

questions.question: What is an infinite loop? How can you prevent it?
keyPoints: Infinite loop has condition that never becomes false
Caused by missing update or wrong condition
Prevention: proper initialization, condition update, termination check

questions.question: Write a Java program to print numbers from 1 to 10 using a for loop.
Explain nested loops with an example.
keyPoints: Nested loop is a loop inside another loop
Inner loop completes all iterations for each outer iteration

questions.question: Write a Java program to calculate the factorial of a number using iteration. Also explain the logic.
keyPoints: Use for loop from 1 to n

questions.question: Write a Java program to display the Fibonacci series up to n terms using appropriate iteration statements.
keyPoints: Use for/while loop for n terms

questions.question: Explain different types of loops in Java with their syntax and when to use each type.
keyPoints: do-while: when at least one execution needed

questions.question: Write a Java program to find whether a number is prime or not using iteration. Explain the algorithm.
keyPoints: If divisible by any number, not prime
Use for loop with condition i*i <= n
Handle edge cases: n<2 not prime, 2 is prime
